title: Dissection and fixation of murine colonic tissue
author: Dante Heredia
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xz6fp9e

steps.title: A ventral midline incision is made...
steps.instruction: A ventral midline incision is made and the whole colon is carefully excised into a Sylguard lined dissection dish.

steps.title: Cut along the mesenteric border until...
steps.instruction: Cut along the mesenteric border until the colonic tube is now a rectanular in shape.

steps.title: Pin the colon at 110% of...
steps.instruction: Pin the colon at 110% of length and width mucosa side up. Gently remove the mucosal layer. Re-pin in a new Sylguard lined dish at 100% ensuring tissue is taught but not stretched.

steps.title: Fix tissue using ice cold Zamboni fixative for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Fix tissue using ice cold Zamboni fixative for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Remove tissue from dish and wash...
steps.instruction: Remove tissue from dish and wash 3 times in PBS for 15 minutes each wash.

steps.title: Cut tissue into 3 sections; oral,...
steps.instruction: Cut tissue into 3 sections; oral, middle and anal (in order to fit on slide) and mount on glass slide using Aquamount mounting media and a glass cover slip.
